Daniela, the rollout had problems. How will you tell the client?
I will say: it seems we have hit a small setback, but we have a workaround in place.
Good. And the timeline?
There appears to be a slight delay, so we might need a little more time. The dates are still tentative.
And the budget? There is a problem there too.
There is a minor shortfall, so we may need to revisit the numbers. One caveat: these are preliminary figures.
Perfect tone. What caused it?
There was a small oversight in the planning. The tradeoff is a little more time for a safer launch.
It seems we have hit a small setback.

Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, listen to one short voice note about softening bad news. When you give a difficult update, the direct version can sound too harsh. The project is late. The test failed. So we use hedging to make it gentler and more diplomatic. There are three easy tools. First, soften the verb. Instead of it is late, say it seems to be a little behind. Instead of we will miss the deadline, say we might miss the deadline. Second, use a softening phrase. There appears to be a small delay. It looks like we may need more time. Third, make the problem sound smaller with words like slight, small, or minor. It is a slight setback, not a disaster. Notice the modals. Might, may, and could make it less certain and less direct. And remember, soften the news, but stay clear and honest. Do not hide the problem. So, use it seems, it might, and a slight, to deliver bad news with diplomacy. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus. I am leaving you a quick voicemail before you update the client about the project. I know there are problems, but please soften the message. Here is how I would frame it. Do not say the rollout failed. Say it seems we have hit a small setback. Do not say we are way behind. Say there appears to be a slight delay in the timeline. For the budget, there is a minor shortfall, so say we may need to revisit the numbers. Mention the drawback honestly, but add the workaround. We have a temporary fallback in place. Add a caveat. These are preliminary findings, so the dates are still tentative. There was an oversight in the planning, and the tradeoff is a little more time for a safer launch. The client will appreciate an honest but diplomatic update. Soften it, but keep it clear. Call me back when you can. Thanks, Daniela. Talk soon.
